qa_pairs.question: When was the Mist compute cluster retired?
qa_pairs.answer: The Mist compute cluster was retired on September 16, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What action should users take after the Mist cluster's retirement?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should transition new work to Trillium. Further guidance is available in the Trillium Quickstart documentation.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Cedar compute cluster retired?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar compute cluster was retired on September 12, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: Do users need to take any action for files stored on Cedar after its retirement?
qa_pairs.answer: No, files stored on Cedar are already available on Fir because the two clusters share the same file systems. No action is required regarding your stored files.

qa_pairs.question: Where should users submit new jobs after the Cedar cluster's retirement?
qa_pairs.answer: Starting September 12, users should submit their jobs to another cluster on the new national infrastructure, including Fir.

qa_pairs.question: When was the Graham compute cluster retired?
qa_pairs.answer: The Graham compute cluster was retired on September 1, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What is the status of files stored on Graham after its retirement?
qa_pairs.answer: Files stored on Graham are already available on Nibi because the two clusters share the same file systems. No action is required regarding your stored files.

qa_pairs.question: Which cluster should users use for new jobs after Graham's retirement?
qa_pairs.answer: Starting September 1, users should submit their jobs to another cluster on the new national infrastructure, including Nibi.

qa_pairs.question: How will the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) be affected by the infrastructure transition?
qa_pairs.answer: The Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) will be impacted by this transition, but the application process remains the same.

qa_pairs.question: When will 2025/26 RAC allocations become available?
qa_pairs.answer: The 2025/26 RAC allocations will be implemented everywhere once all new clusters are in service.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if a user holds both a 2024 and 2025 RAC award during the transition?
qa_pairs.answer: If a user holds both a 2024 and a 2025 RAC award, they will experience a period when neither award is available because the old clusters will mostly be out of service before all new ones are available.

qa_pairs.question: Can users still compute on new clusters before their 2025 RAC allocations are active?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, users will be able to compute with their default allocation (def-xxxxxx) on each new cluster as soon as it goes into service, even before the 2025 RAC allocations become fully available.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find more training opportunities?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can discover more training opportunities through Explora.

qa_pairs.question: What is the 'Introduction to HPC on Alliance Clusters' training about?
qa_pairs.answer: This 3-part webinar series covers an introduction to HPC on Alliance clusters, including hardware, software environment, various programming tools, compiling codes, using Slurm, benchmarking, and managing permissions, with demos and hands-on exercises.

qa_pairs.question: Who provides the 'HPC105: Intro to SciNet and Trillium' course?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'HPC105: Intro to SciNet and Trillium' course is provided by SciNet and taught by the SciNet Education Team.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the 'Migrating to the upgraded national systems' webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: This webinar was designed to address user concerns and questions regarding the major upgrade of Alliance national systems, covering impacts on workflows, job scheduling, file systems, and particularly GPU computing, due to new hardware and increased capacity.
